mission.title: tikv: Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor
mission.description: Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current tikv deployment
steps.description:
Verify your tiup --version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
helm list -n tikv 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working tikv installation.

steps.title: Review tikv configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant tikv configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get configmap -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tikv
```
Using [TiDB Chunk format](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/9627132db25e5cb560ba6ed89c2e39c32edb267a/util/chunk/chunk.go) in the `coprocessor` framework instead of `VectorValue`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor
steps.description:
### What problem does this PR solve?

This PR changes all vectorized functions to have signature like `do_something(a: Option<&Int>, b: Option<&Json>`.

In [RFC: Using chunk format in coprocessor framework](https://github.com/tikv/rfcs/pull/43/files), we proposed a `ChunkedVec` struct to support Chunk-based computing. We plan to:

1. Migrate current co-processor framework to use interface compatible with `ChunkedVec`. 

This is done by adding new functionalities on current `Vec<Option<T>>`. The

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv
kubectl get events -n tikv --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Full chunk-based computing in coprocessor" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A running TiKV cluster (standalone or deployed on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator). TiUP and tikv-ctl are the primary CLI tools for managing TiKV.
